perturbation.original_text: A Confidential Information@ shall mean any and all information that a Party may reasonably expect to remain confidential and not shared with the general public at any time or that information that is protected by law.
perturbation.changed_text: A Confidential Information@ shall mean any and all information that a Party deems confidential, regardless of its accessibility to the general public or whether it is protected by law.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' by stating that any information a party 'deems' confidential is considered as such, irrespective of its public accessibility or legal protection. This conflicts with the implicit understanding that confidential information typically involves an expectation of privacy and legal safeguards. Now the definition of confidential information is entirely subjective and potentially includes publicly known information, which is a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is anticipated that the Business Affiliate will provide services both for clients of the Business Affiliate and UDC that involve the use and disclosure of Confidential Information (the "Services"). Except as otherwise specified herein, the Parties may make any and all uses of Confidential Information necessary to perform the Services. Additionally, the Parties may disclose Confidential Information for the purposes authorized by this Agreement only (a) to its employees, subcontractors and agents, in accordance with this Agreement, or (b) as directed by the other Party. The Parties expressly agree that any and all uses or disclosures of the Confidential Information by a Party will be done in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the provisions of all applicable federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include anything a party 'deems' confidential, irrespective of public accessibility or legal protection. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which anticipates the use and disclosure of Confidential Information specifically for services involving clients of the Business Affiliate and UDC and emphasizes compliance with laws and regulations. If 'Confidential Information' can now include publicly available information, the stipulations regarding its use and disclosure become meaningless and create a contradiction. The need to comply with laws related to confidential information is rendered ambiguous when the definition includes items with no legal protection.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect until either Party informs the other Party with thirty (30) day prior written notice of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect unless UDC decides otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: This modification allows UDC to terminate the agreement at will without prior notice, directly contradicting the original provision requiring a 30-day written notice. This creates ambiguity about the actual term of the agreement and undermines the original provision's intent of providing a guaranteed notice period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UDC may immediately terminate this Agreement without notice and any related agreement if it determines that the Business Affiliate has breached a material provision of this Agreement or if the activities contemplated under this Agreement will result in a violation of any federal or state law or if such Services would, in the opinion of UDC and its counsel. Alternatively, UDC may choose to: (i) provide the Business Affiliate with ten (10) days written notice of the existence of an alleged material breach; and (ii) afford the Business Affiliate an opportunity to cure said alleged material breach upon mutually agreeable terms. Failure to cure in the manner set forth in this paragraph is grounds for the immediate termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows UDC to terminate the agreement 'unless UDC decides otherwise,' effectively removing the 30-day written notice requirement. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which outlines specific conditions for termination, including instances where notice is required and instances where it is not. The 'Changed Text' creates a scenario where UDC can terminate at will without any notice, conflicting with the scenarios where a 10-day notice and opportunity to cure are mandated. This creates an ambiguity regarding the actual term of the agreement and whether UDC is bound by any notice period as originally stated, thus violating the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party represents and warrants to the other Party that all of its employees, agents, representatives and members of its work force, whose services may be used to fulfill obligations under this Agreement, are or shall be appropriately informed of the terms of this Agreement and are under legal obligation to comply fully with all provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party represents that some of its employees, agents, representatives and members of its work force, whose services may be used to fulfill obligations under this Agreement, may be informed of the terms of this Agreement; however, there is no guarantee that they will be under legal obligation to comply fully with all provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This amendment introduces uncertainty by weakening the representation and warranty concerning employee compliance. By changing 'all' to 'some' and removing the assurance of legal obligation, it creates doubt about whether all relevant personnel are actually bound by the agreement's terms. Now only some employees may be informed of the terms of the Agreement, this contradicts Section 3(d) which states that all employees receiving confidential information under this Agreement to agree in writing to adhere to the same restrictions and conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Require all of its employees, representatives, subcontractors or agents that receive or use or have access to Confidential Information under this Agreement to agree in writing to adhere to the same restrictions and conditions on the use and/or disclosure of Confidential Information that apply herein, including the obligation to return or destroy the Confidential Information as provided under paragraph g of this Section 3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the requirement for all employees to comply with the agreement, stating that only 'some' may be informed and there's no guarantee of legal obligation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that 'all' employees with access to confidential information must agree in writing to adhere to the agreement's restrictions. The change introduces ambiguity about which employees are bound, creating a clear conflict with the obligation to ensure all relevant personnel agree to confidentiality terms.
